hard.next_step: 6/16MF - account transitioned to MikeF. Intro EM sent to Rachel VP Supply Chain re: project status. Sounding as though Oracle bias is a blocker but confirming. 12/10 RH MM connected with Rachel. Update: "This process has been extensive, and it seems time is not in our favour in many aspects due to the parallel Oracle implementation we are navigating, we are now on phase 3. We are having several discussions internally lately and will likely come to a decision to collapse the RFP activities due to competing priorities, which is creating capacity bottleneck. This is not an official note, but rather an indication on where I see these things going as of now… We have a meeting scheduled for the last week of October and will know better where we are going with this and will then provide a formal notification on what we will do next. Again, we apologize for the lengthy process, and we are committed to provide an update on what is next as soon as we reach an agreement on the way forward.
hard.ais_why:
• The opportunity description and discovery notes both state that Davie is "exploring Zycus' Gen AI capabilities, particularly in sourcing and vendor management". That buyer-stated interest is on record. The Feb 13 2025 demo notes confirm Zycus showed generative AI capabilities for RFP creation and supplier response evaluation, and Maria from legal engaged on CLM AI features.
• Merlin and Agentic AI are included in Products, but ANA and Merlin Intake are absent. AI importance is rated 'Important' rather than 'Very Critical'. The buyer's questions concentrated on Oracle integration, data residency in Canada and out-of-the-box fit. The AI thread was real but not the lead requirement.
• The deal has stalled. A December 2024 update notes Davie is likely to collapse the RFP activities because of competing priorities, with a parallel Oracle Fusion implementation taking precedence. The AI interest exists, but the capacity bottleneck and ERP priorities are overshadowing it, so the score stays on the Curious side of the line.

north_star_verdict.headline: Davie has effectively chosen Oracle for source-to-contract: the 17 Jun 2026 buyer email states they are 'currently in the process of implementing the S2C modules within our Oracle ERP platform,' and Rachel Lemos confirmed on 16 Jun 2026 that last year's RFP proponents were already told the results and 'our decision on the way forward.' After ~17 months still in Formal Evaluation with no pricing, no business case and no live working session since the April 2025 demo, this reads as a loss to Oracle rather than a live deal.
north_star_verdict.math: Close 2026-10-14 is 100 days out (today 2026-07-06). Created 2025-01-29, so ~17 months in cycle and still in Formal Evaluation, qualified 2025-04-21. Last real working session was the April 2025 on-site demo (~14 months ago). The only 2026 touch is the 16-17 Jun email exchange after the account moved to Mike Flowers on 2026-06-11, and it carries a buyer statement that they are implementing Oracle S2C. No movement toward Validation / Proposal / Negotiation; CloseDate has slipped five times (2025-10-29 -> 2025-11-20 -> 2026-03-20 -> 2026-04-30 -> 2026-08-30 -> 2026-10-14) with no underlying progress.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Omitted (or Pipeline at most): Best Case is indefensible. The buyer says they are implementing Oracle's S2C modules, an award decision was already communicated to RFP proponents, there is no quantified business case, no pricing conversation ever occurred, and the economic buyer has cooled to signalling RFP collapse.
evidence: SF activity 2026-06-17 (buyer email): 'We are currently in the process of implementing the S2C modules within our Oracle ERP platform, for which the P2P modules are already live.'
SF activity 2026-06-16 (Rachel Lemos email): 'We had issued communication to all Proponents that participated in the RFP last year with re results and our decision on the way forward.'
SF Next_Step__c (Rachel Lemos, 10 Dec 2025): buyer indicated a likely decision to collapse RFP activities due to competing Oracle priorities creating a capacity bottleneck.
SF Next_Step__c owner note (Mike Flowers, 16 Jun 2026): 'Sounding as though Oracle bias is a blocker but confirming.'
SF LastActivityDate 2026-06-17: only 2026 touch is a status email after the account transitioned to Mike Flowers on 2026-06-11; last live buyer working session was the S2C demo in April 2025.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists after 17 months. Across the demo workshop (13 Feb 2025) and the clarification session (11 Apr 2025) the buyer articulated only qualitative pain: Rachel Lemos said 'it's so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be' and 'we're far away from where we want to be' (11 Apr 2025). No savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time or ROI figure was ever stated, and no pricing conversation has ever taken place. That missing financial justification is precisely why the buyer can defer procurement into the Oracle Fusion program without a business consequence. Quantifying the cost of the manual status quo and of waiting would have been the move, but with Oracle S2C now being implemented it is likely too late to reopen on value alone.

economic_buyer.narrative: Rachel Lemos, VP of Supply Chain, is the economic buyer and controlled framing throughout: on 13 Feb 2025 she set scope ('what we're looking for... is to automate the sourcing process') and confirmed Procure-to-Pay out of scope. She was an enthusiastic early sponsor ('we're very excited about this project... decision made very soon', 11 Apr 2025), but her 10 Dec 2025 note shifted to signalling a probable RFP collapse and her 16 Jun 2026 email confirms the RFP result was already communicated to proponents. Her budget authority is clear from Salesforce (VP of Supply Chain, primary buyer contact), but she is now cooling and steering the account toward Oracle S2C. No commercial or pricing conversation was ever held with her.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Rachel Lemos, VP of Supply Chain)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are known from the calls even though they were never formally documented to us: out-of-the-box fit with minimal customization ('a solution that has a lot of out of the box functionalities that we don't need to be customizing', Rachel Lemos, 03 Feb 2025), Oracle-compatibility ('solution must be compatible with Oracle', Rachel Lemos, 03 Feb 2025), Canadian data residency for federal-government work (Richard Hunsinger flagged it 11 Apr 2025, pending VP IT confirmation), CLM authoring depth for legal (Maria Dinu, 13 Feb 2025), and mandatory/scored gating with disqualification logic and NDA gating in sourcing (Rachel Lemos, 13 Feb 2025). Oracle-compatibility and low-customization criteria now favour the incumbent ERP, which is a core reason Zycus is losing the scope.
sources: Demo workshop 13 Feb 2025 (Maria Dinu, Rachel Lemos)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer ran a formal RFP-led evaluation: an NDA gate before RFP content access, cross-functional sign-off on RFP sections (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle described interfunctional approval flows where each department approves its own section, 11 Apr 2025), and a demo cohort spanning supply chain, legal and VP IT. Rachel Lemos set a decision checkpoint for the last week of October 2025 and promised a formal notification afterward (SF Next_Step__c, 10 Dec 2025). That process collided with the Oracle Fusion phase-3 rollout, and by 16-17 Jun 2026 the outcome had resolved against a standalone S2C purchase: results were communicated to RFP proponents and Oracle S2C modules are being implemented. The process is effectively concluded, not mid-flight.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics ever started: no MSA, Order Form, SOW or redline stage was reached, so there is nothing to advance on paper. What is known is context: the evaluation ran under a mutual NDA (Richard Hunsinger confirmed 'we're under NDA', 11 Apr 2025, with Zycus legal proposing mutual language on the buyer's one-sided NDA), and ~90% of Davie's work is Canadian federal government, imposing Canadian-content, security-clearance and data-residency requirements. The two paper prerequisites that were never closed, Canadian data-residency confirmation (owned by the relevant stakeholder Citone, pending since 11 Apr 2025) and AI/data-security assurance, are now moot because the S2C award went to the relevant stakeholder paper would require a fresh, re-qualified opportunity.
sources: Demo workshop 13 Feb 2025 (Richard Hunsinger, Maria Dinu)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and confirmed, and it remains the one durable asset for any re-entry. Sourcing and vendor management run through email with no formal process: 'the vendor management process is mostly done manually... through email' producing a 'bad habit to always go with the same vendors' (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, 11 Apr 2025). Rachel Lemos called the current state 'so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be' and described a hand-worked 'dirty database' of supplier registrations (11 Apr 2025). Legal wants contract authoring and lifecycle visibility across deadlines and amendments (Maria Dinu, 13 Feb 2025). Pain owners are supply chain (Rachel Lemos / Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle) and legal (Maria Dinu). The pain is real but was never quantified and is now being addressed via Oracle rather than Zycus.
sources: Clarification session 11 Apr 2025 (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, Rachel Lemos)

champion.narrative: Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, Project Procurement Manager, was a developing champion: he drove demo scheduling and logistics and called the project 'dear to my heart as this will help to improve our actual process' (11 Apr 2025), and Rachel Lemos was an enthusiastic early sponsor. But nothing developed after April 2025: neither drove the deal forward, Rachel's December note shifted toward collapse, and the June 2026 exchange routes the status question back through JP only as an administrative contact, not as an advocate. No internal voice ever countered the Oracle default. With Oracle S2C now being implemented, the champion is effectively inactive and did not or could not fight for a standalone Zycus decision.
sources: Clarification session 11 Apr 2025 (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, Rachel Lemos)

competition.narrative: The competition was never a rival best-of-breed S2P vendor; it was Oracle plus inertia, and Oracle has won the S2C scope. Davie uses Oracle for services and was mid-implementation of Oracle Fusion (Rachel Lemos, 03 and 13 Feb 2025); the buyer required any solution be 'compatible with the relevant stakeholder 10 Dec 2025 the Oracle phase-3 program was the competing priority threatening RFP collapse, and the 17 Jun 2026 buyer email confirms Davie is now implementing the S2C modules inside Oracle (P2P already live). The wedge that could have won, deep sourcing / CLM depth plus Merlin AI and Canadian-government workflow that ERP-native procurement cannot match, was never converted into a quantified, phased proposal, so Oracle's low-customization, single-platform argument prevailed.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved decisively against Zycus this period. The account transitioned to Mike Flowers on 11 Jun 2026, who re-introduced himself cold to Rachel Lemos; her 16 Jun reply confirmed the RFP result was already communicated to proponents, and the 17 Jun buyer email confirmed Davie is implementing Oracle's S2C modules. This follows a long stall since the April 2025 demo, the 10 Dec 2025 RFP-collapse warning, and five close-date slips through 2025-2026 with no underlying progress.

items.change: Buyer confirmed they are implementing S2C modules within Oracle ERP (P2P already live), indicating Oracle won the source-to-contract scope
Rachel Lemos confirmed last year's RFP proponents were already told the results and the decision on the way forward
Account ownership transitioned to Mike Flowers; new owner re-introduced to Rachel Lemos and awaiting Jean-Philippe on S2P project status
CloseDate pushed 2026-08-30 -> 2026-10-14 with no underlying deal progression
Buyer signalled likely collapse of RFP activities due to parallel Oracle Fusion phase-3 implementation and capacity bottleneck

competitive_position.summary: Single dominant threat, now realised: Oracle. Davie was mid-implementation of Oracle Fusion throughout, required Oracle-compatibility, and by 17 Jun 2026 stated it is implementing the S2C modules inside Oracle (P2P already live), so the source-to-contract award effectively went to Oracle by default and single-platform simplicity, not to a competing best-of-breed vendor. The secondary threat, do-nothing / deferring procurement into the ERP rollout, has resolved into the same Oracle outcome. Our only surviving wedge is repositioning as a best-of-breed sourcing + CLM + Merlin AI layer on top of Oracle for a future phase, led through VP IT Jeremy Citone and legal (Maria Dinu).

competitors.name: Oracle Fusion (native procurement modules)
competitors.quote: We are currently in the process of implementing the S2C modules within our Oracle ERP platform, for which the P2P modules are already live.
competitors.source: SF activity 2026-06-17 (buyer email); SF Next_Step__c 10 Dec 2025; Demo workshop 13 Feb 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe Zycus as the best-of-breed procurement layer ON Oracle (not a replacement): a phased post-go-live deployment using our native Oracle connectors, leading with Merlin AI sourcing, deep CLM authoring for legal, and Canadian-government workflow depth Oracle native cannot match; carry an Oracle-integration reference to VP IT Jeremy Citone.

competitors.name: Do nothing / defer procurement into the Oracle rollout
competitors.quote: we will likely come to a decision to collapse the RFP activities due to competing priorities, which is creating capacity bottleneck.
competitors.how_we_win: Now largely subsumed by the Oracle S2C decision; the only re-entry is to tie a light, phased first-scope (CLM for legal + strategic sourcing) to the government-compliance clock so a Zycus layer survives alongside the Oracle program.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Davie's stated expectations, out-of-the-box fit with minimal customization, Oracle-compatibility, Canadian data residency, and strong CLM for legal, tilted the field toward the incumbent ERP. Zycus demonstrated strong functional fit on sourcing, CLM and GenAI, but never neutralized the Oracle-compatibility and single-platform preference, and never resolved data residency, which left us exposed on the criteria that mattered most.

items.quote: we're looking for a solution that has a lot of out of the box functionalities that we don't need to be customizing to fit our needs
solution must be compatible with Oracle... it's our ERP of choice

items.criterion: Canadian data residency for federal-government work
items.quote: I would think you're going to require Canadian data hosting because of the Canadian government's data residency requirements
items.source: Clarification session 11 Apr 2025 (Richard Hunsinger); never confirmed by VP IT

items.criterion: Full CLM authoring and lifecycle for legal
items.quote: we are really looking into a full contract life cycle management software that can give us a better understanding of what contracts we have and the deadlines

items.deliverable: Get Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle to confirm in writing where the S2P/S2C project stands and the formal RFP outcome
items.grounding_quote: We'll wait to hear back from Jean-Philippe on where the S2P project stands.

items.quote: We had issued communication to all Proponents that participated in the RFP last year with re results and our decision on the way forward.
it's so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be
I need our VP of ID to confirm it... IT to confirm this point

best_practice_check.summary: This is a losing/lost EARLY-stage deal, so the win-strategy levers are about confirming the loss cleanly and preserving a best-of-breed-on-Oracle re-entry, not chasing a dead RFP.
flags: Confirm the RFP outcome in writing before investing further: the 17 Jun buyer email says Oracle S2C is being implemented, so qualify out or reposition rather than continuing to forecast Best Case.

Reposition from ERP-replacement to best-of-breed-on-Oracle: lead with Merlin AI sourcing and deep CLM authoring that Oracle Fusion native lacks, carried to the relevant stakeholder Citone as a complementary layer.
Rebuild a cold, single-threaded relationship: only the new owner has touched Rachel and JP in 2026; multi-thread to Maria Dinu (legal CLM) to find a live legal-led sub-scope Oracle does not cover.

items.name: Rachel Lemos
items.title: VP of Supply Chain
items.risk: Shifted from enthusiastic sponsor to steering the S2C scope into Oracle; controls budget but is no longer buying Zycus

items.name: Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle
items.title: Project Procurement Manager
items.risk: Developing champion who never converted to active advocacy; now only the administrative routing point for the status question

items.name: Jeremy Citone
items.title: Vice President Information Technology
items.risk: Owns the unresolved data-residency decision and the Oracle Fusion program; the Oracle bias sits in his domain and he was never engaged directly

items.name: Maria Dinu
items.title: Legal Counsel
items.risk: Legal CLM requirements unaddressed since the February 2025 demo; a possible legal-led re-entry point Oracle does not fully cover

champion_strength.summary: Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle was a developing champion who drove demo logistics and called the project 'dear to my heart' (11 Apr 2025), but showed no proactive advocacy after April 2025 and never fought the Oracle default. With Davie now implementing Oracle S2C, he is effectively an inactive administrative contact rather than an advocate.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus positioned Merlin GenAI and Agentic AI strongly in the demos (RFP question generation, contract OCR/digitization, supplier scoring, Teams-embedded Merlin intake), and the buyer showed genuine appetite gated on data security. But AI was never the lead requirement and did not overcome the Oracle-compatibility and single-platform preference, so strong AI positioning did not change the outcome.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 / Moderate is consistent with the calls: Rachel Lemos said 'there is appetite for us to streamline and use AI, without the risks' (11 Apr 2025), but the appetite is conditioned on keeping government data secure and AI ranked behind Oracle-fit, data residency and out-of-the-box functionality. Real but secondary interest, not a lead buying driver.

items.detail: Economic buyer Rachel Lemos has steered the S2C scope into the Oracle Fusion program; no Zycus advocate countered the ERP default
Buyer required Oracle compatibility and single-platform simplicity; implementing Oracle's own S2C modules removes Zycus's differentiation on that criterion
items.source: Demo discovery workshop 03 Feb 2025; SF activity 2026-06-17

items.detail: No quantified business case or pricing conversation in 17 months, making Best Case indefensible and the project easy to fold into the ERP rollout
Parallel Oracle Fusion phase-3 rollout created a capacity bottleneck that collapsed the standalone S2C RFP

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold and the buyer has signalled a decision against a standalone Zycus purchase. The only 2026 contact is a June status email, and it carries the buyer's statement that Oracle S2C is being implemented.
items: No live buyer working session since the April 2025 demo (~14 months).
Buyer implementing Oracle S2C modules per 17 Jun 2026 email.
RFP results already communicated to proponents per 16 Jun 2026 email.

items.action: Email Jean-Philippe directly to confirm in writing the RFP outcome and whether Oracle S2C is now the chosen path.
items.trigger: Buyer said they are implementing Oracle S2C modules and that RFP results were already communicated to proponents.
items.expected_effect: Establishes win/loss truth so the deal is qualified out of Best Case or repositioned, instead of forecasting a dead RFP.

items.action: Pitch Rachel a phased best-of-breed sourcing and CLM layer on Oracle for a post-Fusion go-live window.
items.trigger: Buyer chose Oracle for S2C but Oracle-native procurement lacks Zycus sourcing and CLM depth.
items.expected_effect: Keeps a Zycus complementary-layer opportunity alive rather than a total loss, using native Oracle connectors.

items.action: Reconnect Maria Dinu on standalone CLM authoring value that Oracle contract handling does not cover.
items.trigger: Legal wanted full contract lifecycle management with deadlines and amendment tracking, unaddressed since Feb 2025.
items.expected_effect: Opens a legal-led re-entry sub-scope where Zycus differentiates from Oracle native.

items.action: Send Jeremy Citone an Oracle-integration reference and Canadian data-residency proof to preserve Zycus as a complementary layer.
items.trigger: VP IT owns the Oracle program and the never-answered data-residency question that shaped the criteria.
items.expected_effect: Neutralizes the integration objection and positions Zycus for a future phase within the IT-owned Oracle landscape.

critical_signals.text: On 17 June Davie said it is now implementing the source-to-contract modules inside its Oracle ERP, signalling Oracle won the S2C scope.
The only 2026 contact was a June status email after the account changed owners, in which Rachel confirmed last year's RFP proponents were already told the decision on the way forward.
The account transitioned to Mike Flowers on 11 June, who is re-introducing himself cold to Rachel and Jean-Philippe with no prior relationship.
No pricing or ROI conversation ever occurred in 17 months, leaving the Best Case forecast indefensible.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer is implementing Oracle S2C modules and required Oracle compatibility and out-of-the-box fit; no Zycus-specific commercial next step was ever requested (SF activity 2026-06-17; Demo discovery workshop 03 Feb 2025).

business_case.evidence: Only qualitative pain ('it's so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be', Rachel Lemos 11 Apr 2025); no savings, headcount, cycle-time or ROI figure ever stated.

scope_change.from: Source-to-Contract (iSource, iContract, iSupplier) with Merlin/Agentic AI; P2P out of scope
scope_change.to: Source-to-Contract (iSource, iContract, iSupplier) with Merlin/Agentic AI; P2P out of scope
scope_change.detail: Scope held stable across the cycle at standalone S2C with P2P explicitly out of scope; the change is not scope narrowing but the buyer moving the same S2C scope onto Oracle.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 20 is carried by exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 51 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 4 is anchored on the stage win (20), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 51, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: Chantier Davie Canada — Formal Evaluation · $225K · closes 14 Oct
cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

gaps: No live buyer working session or transcript since April 2025; the two 2025 pre-demo calls carry no transcript this sweep, so the 2026 read rests on the June email exchange.
Formal written RFP outcome not on record; the buyer references a communicated decision but the award document/vendor is not directly confirmed.
No pricing, ROI or quantified business case ever captured.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'VP IT Jeremy')
